description_long: This is a dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with a robust fuselage and a low-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft features prominent white star insignias and the number '19' on the fuselage and tail, indicating a World War II-era U.S. Navy or Marine Corps design. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a wire antenna extending from the tail to the fuselage. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is streamlined for aerodynamic efficiency and combat maneuverability.
description: A dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with white star insignias, an enclosed cockpit, and a streamlined fuselage marked '19'. Features a low-mounted straight wing and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the propeller at the nose, the cockpit canopy, and the prominent '19' and star insignias.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, the tail section, and the star insignia on the left wing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail, the '19' markings, and the wire antenna extending from the tail to the fuselage.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, the nose, and the symmetrical wings with visible insignia on the right wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on design cues.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
